candidate_summary: 3-channel attention-head triplet (1 drone-head + 2 melody-heads) per layer phase-locked against shared drone-reference embedding; drone-head produces continuous EMA reference sustained across context; melody-heads add phase-locked offset; circular-breathing-style persistent drone KV; phase-coherence loss penalises drift.
content_words: tumbu drone mancosa melody pipe phase
shared drone-reference embedding continuous across context
circular-breathing sustained KV reference no recomputation

results.name: TransMLA Multi-Head Latent Attention (2502.07864)
results.description: latent KV serves as shared large head interacting with all query/output heads; decoupled RoPE positional embedding.
results.rationale: Shared latent KV interacts with multiple heads, conceptually similar to a shared drone reference, but lacks explicit triplet 1+2 split, phase-coherence loss, EMA-based long-horizon drone, and circular-breathing persistent KV semantics.

results.name: DuoAttention Retrieval Streaming Heads (2410.10819)
results.description: dual-channel attention; retrieval heads with full KV vs streaming heads with constant-length KV + attention sink anchor.
results.rationale: Dual-channel head split with persistent anchor resembles drone+melody two-channel structure and sustained reference KV, but uses 2 channels not a 1+2 triplet, no phase-locking offset, no EMA drone, no coherence loss.

results.name: Enabling DeepSeek MLA Any Architecture (ACL 2025)
results.description: MLA latent KV as shared reference across all attention heads; cross-head synchronization through compressed latent.
results.rationale: Cross-head synchronization via shared latent reference is conceptually adjacent to drone-reference phase-locking, but lacks explicit triplet structure, phase offsets, EMA continuity, and drift penalization loss.

results.name: Attention Heads of LLMs Survey (PMC 2025)
results.description: coherence heads ensure linguistic consistency; distinct heads specialize for rule location, fact processing, decision-making.
results.rationale: Mentions coherence heads and head specialization, conceptually distant from phase-locked triplet with EMA drone reference and circular-breathing KV.

results.name: LongHeads Multi-Head Long Context (2402.10685)
results.description: chunk selection by query-key correlation distributes context chunks across heads.
results.rationale: Multi-head distribution of context chunks; no shared drone reference, no triplet, no phase-coherence loss, no persistent EMA.

results.name: Attention Heads of LLMs Survey (2409.03752)
results.description: comprehensive survey of attention head specialization.
results.rationale: General head specialization survey; no specific phase-locked drone+melody triplet mechanism or coherence-loss training.

results.name: Unifying MoE Multi-Head Latent Attention (2508.01261)
results.description: shared latent attention reference + MoE; cross-head and cross-expert synchronization via shared latent.
results.rationale: Shared latent reference enabling cross-head synchronization is functionally similar to drone-reference embedding, but no triplet 1+2 split, no EMA-long drone, no phase-coherence drift loss, no circular-breathing KV.

results.name: Attention KV Cache Diffusion LLMs (2510.14973)
results.description: KV cache persistence across diffusion-LLM context; shared persistent reference state.
results.rationale: Persistent KV cache as shared reference state aligns with circular-breathing sustained drone KV, but lacks phase-locked triplet head structure and coherence loss penalising drift.

decision_rationale: No prior-art result reaches the hit threshold. Closest analogs (TransMLA, DuoAttention, MLA-Any, MoE-MLA, Diffusion-KV) share the shared/persistent latent-reference idea (sem/func ~0.55-0.6) but none combine the specific triplet 1-drone+2-melody phase-locking, EMA-long drone reference, circular-breathing persistent drone KV, and phase-coherence drift loss. All scores stay below kw>=2, sem>=0.7, func>=0.7. Verdict: PASS.
primary_disagreement_flag: Verdict-level disagreement: verifier PASS (0 hits) vs primary FAIL (8 hits). Verifier judged triplet-with-EMA-drone + phase-coherence drift loss specific enough to clear all shared-latent-KV adjacencies. Primary scored shared-latent-KV (MLA/MoE-MLA/DuoAttention) as functional ≥ 0.7. R477 marked PassC borderline; flagged for potential future Phase-0 audit if EMA-drone-triplet phase-coherence territory becomes interesting.
